prose:
Method–provenance summary (for full reproducibility)
Property: Atom-condensed Fukui indices (f⁺ for electrophilic attack, f⁻ for nucleophilic attack, dual descriptor f⁰)
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting) in PySCF  (chemkit ‘dft’ backend)
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (pyridine.xyz, 11 atoms) used as-is; no further optimisation (single-point energies for the N, N±1 states)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet parent (Q = 0, M = 1); cation state Q = +1, M = 2; anion state Q = −1, M = 2
Software: chemkit  (mcp_server) calling PySCF; exact four-center integrals (density fitting OFF)
Convergence & caveats: All three SCF calculations converged; integrity.trustworthy = true.  Engine issued three warnings (verbatim below).  Sums of f⁺ and f⁻ each equal 1.0000 indicating charge conservation holds.  Live engine output was streamed to the .out file announced when the job started (see your session log); result JSON written to /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpcrdvrdak/live.json .
